landing.badgeNew: New: AI flashcards + study pet in one place
landing.headline: Study smarter with your new best friend
landing.subheadline: Turn any video, PDF or notes into flashcards in seconds. Grow your pet. Ace your exams.

features.aiBody: Paste notes, upload PDF or paste YouTube URL — get flashcards, summaries and quizzes instantly.
features.petBody: Your axolotl grows as you study. Keep it happy and unlock rewards.
features.groupsBody: Create study groups, share decks, compete on leaderboards.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: From notes to flashcards in minutes — then your pet keeps you consistent.
how.step1Title: Step 1 Paste your notes or YouTube URL
how.step1Body: Drop in any topic: lecture notes, revision guides, or a YouTube link.
how.step2Title: Step 2 Get flashcards, summary and quiz
how.step2Body: Bloom generates study-ready materials you can review right away.
how.step3Title: Step 3 Study with your pet and track progress
how.step3Body: Earn coins, keep your streak, and watch your axolotl grow.

facts.1: Honey never spoils; archaeologists have tasted 3,000-year-old honey.
facts.2: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
facts.3: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
facts.4: Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
facts.5: The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer heat.
facts.6: Sharks have been around longer than trees.
facts.7: Your brain uses roughly 20% of your body’s energy.
facts.8: A bolt of lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun.
facts.9: Some turtles can breathe through their butts during winter.
facts.10: The shortest war in history lasted about 38 minutes.
facts.11: Cleopatra lived closer to the Moon landing than to the pyramids.
facts.12: There are more possible chess games than atoms in the observable universe.
facts.13: Wombat poop is cube-shaped to stop it rolling away.
facts.14: A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
facts.15: The human stomach gets a new lining every few days.
facts.16: An asteroid impact helped wipe out the dinosaurs 66 million years ago.
facts.17: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.
facts.18: Ants can carry objects many times their own weight.
facts.19: Some frogs freeze solid in winter and thaw back to life in spring.
facts.20: The first computer bug was an actual moth found in 1947.
facts.21: A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh billions of tons on Earth.
facts.22: The Moon is moving away from Earth by about 3.8 cm each year.
facts.23: Dolphins have signature whistles that act like names.
facts.24: The smell of rain is caused partly by a molecule called geosmin.
facts.25: Spiders can’t fly, but many “balloon” on silk threads to travel.
facts.26: Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
facts.27: Penguins propose with pebbles in some species.
facts.28: The longest English word without a vowel is “rhythms”.
facts.29: The coldest place in the universe may be the Boomerang Nebula.
facts.30: A single cloud can weigh more than a million pounds.
facts.31: The “fear center” in your brain is the amygdala.
facts.32: Your eyes can’t see the world in “high definition”; your brain fills gaps.
facts.33: The first Olympic Games were held in Olympia in 776 BCE.
facts.34: The first pizza delivery is recorded in Naples in 1889.
facts.35: Cats can make over 100 vocal sounds; dogs make around 10.
facts.36: Some mushrooms can glow in the dark (bioluminescence).
facts.37: A snail can sleep for up to three years in harsh conditions.
facts.38: The heart of a blue whale is about the size of a small car.
facts.39: Cows have best friends and get stressed when separated.
facts.40: The Amazon rainforest produces a significant share of Earth’s oxygen.
facts.41: The word “robot” comes from a Czech word meaning forced labor.
facts.42: The first printed book was produced in China, not Europe.
facts.43: A crocodile can’t stick its tongue out.
facts.44: The hottest chili pepper can feel like being burned due to capsaicin.
facts.45: The human body has more bacterial cells than human cells (by count).
facts.46: A violin’s sound comes largely from its wooden body, not just strings.
facts.47: Saturn could float in water because it’s less dense than water.
facts.49: The strongest muscle by weight is often said to be the jaw muscle.
facts.50: The Sahara Desert has had snowstorms, even though it is extremely dry.
